summary: Full regression for multi-outlet iteration. Rewrote backend_test.py to cover the new routes (/api/business, /api/business/{id}/queue/*, /api/business/{id}/stats, /api/business/{id}/analytics, /api/public/business/{id}/display) and the new auth/me shape {user, businesses:[]}. 19/19 pytest tests pass. Playwright UI verified: login, dashboard outlet-redirect, walk-in dialog, call-next, online toggle, Outlets listing + create+navigate, Analytics (4 stat cards + recharts bar chart + heatmap + range selector), and public Display page (no auth). Outlet switcher dropdown present on DashboardHeader.
action_items: Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md: admin email is admin@go-next.in (not admin@gonext.com); auth/me now returns {user, businesses:[...]}; queue endpoints are under /api/business/{id}/queue/*; seeded outlets are demo-salon and demo-salon-andheri; new frontend routes include /dashboard/outlets, /dashboard/:businessId/analytics, /display/:businessId.

Consider adding data-testid to walk-in button/inputs, outlet switcher option items, call-next, no-show, online toggle, new-outlet form fields, analytics stat cards/range selector, and display station/clock to make future tests robust (currently had to fall back on text/role selectors).
(Optional) Rate-limit /api/public/business/{id}/join to prevent abuse in production.
critical_code_review_comments: analytics: completed_today / no_show_today use $regex on ISO string — works but an indexed finished_at_date field would scale better.
public display endpoint has no caching headers; for a TV screen polling every 3s, consider Cache-Control: no-store and/or an ETag.

CORS config combines allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True via allow_origin_regex='.*' — functional but non-standard; browsers normally reject credentials with wildcard origin. Works here because allow_origin_regex reflects the request origin, but worth documenting.
On startup the legacy unique-index drop block is good; keep it until all envs are migrated.
No rate-limiting on /api/auth/login — brute-force lockout is NOT implemented (playbook asks for lockout after 5 fails).
Password hash is bcrypt ($2b$) and httpOnly cookie is set correctly on login — auth playbook basics satisfied.

Seed admin is idempotent on email-exists (skips) but does NOT update password if ADMIN_PASSWORD env was changed — minor deviation from playbook 'seed_admin updates existing admin if password changed'.
auth/me 401 on landing still emits a console error; can be silenced in AuthContext by treating 401 as 'unauthenticated' without logging.
test_credentials: admin@go-next.in / admin123 (seeded). Seeded outlets: demo-salon, demo-salon-andheri.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed walk-ins and one UI_TEST_Outlet via Playwright. These linger but are harmless; pytest cleans up most via delete flows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py now targets the new multi-outlet API. Key fixtures: owner_session (admin@go-next.in) and other_owner_session (registers fresh user to test cross-owner 404). Analytics test seeds 2 completed + 1 no_show on demo-salon-andheri to verify aggregation. Playwright selectors rely mostly on text/role; add data-testid attributes suggested above to stabilize future runs. Display page and all /api/public/* endpoints are correctly unauthenticated.
rca of the issue: N/A - no regressions found. Previous iteration's tests were referencing removed routes (/api/queue/manage/*) and wrong admin email (admin@gonext.com); backend_test.py has been rewritten to match the new contract.
